one_line_takeaway: A vision paper that recasts artificial + channel noise as a diffusion forward process so the reverse process can defend semantic-communication links against eavesdropping (Eve's MI ~0 / accuracy ~0.2) and jamming (CJC + fine denoising restores near-no-jamming MSE), demonstrated only on Fashion-MNIST over AWGN channels.
key_finding: Diffusion denoising can simultaneously protect against eavesdropping and recover from jamming when both artificial and channel noise are cast as the diffusion forward process. Eavesdropping (no Eve knowledge): the AN module drives Eve's privacy-leakage MI to near zero while the denoising module cuts Alice-Bob communication MSE and keeps perceptibility MSE well below the all-power-to-AN upper bound (covertness preserved). Eavesdropping (Eve known): adversarial-attack AN drops Eve's classification accuracy to ~0.2 (near-ineffective) while perceptibility MSE stays below 0.1 (transmitter output power normalized to 1), and the denoised Alice-Bob link outperforms the original system. Jamming: for high-power pulse jamming (JSR 40 dB) Bob fails outright; direct diffusion reverse process helps but leaves high MSE, whereas CJC followed by fine diffusion denoising brings MSE close to the no-jamming level. For low-power adversarial jamming (JSR -10 dB) the diffusion reverse process sharply improves MSE, more so as SNR decreases.
core_contribution: A magazine-style framework (position/vision article with illustrative case studies) that repurposes diffusion models to secure the DEPLOYMENT phase of semantic communication against two physical/semantic-layer threats: eavesdropping and jamming. The central idea is to treat BOTH the actively injected artificial noise (AN) and the passively introduced natural channel noise as the FORWARD process of a diffusion model, and then use the diffusion REVERSE process at the legitimate receiver (Bob) to denoise and recover the transmitted meaning. Two concrete framework instances are proposed: (1) an eavesdropping-aware framework combining an AN-generation module (power allocation + noise design) at Alice with a denoising module (time-step estimation + noise estimation) in front of Bob; and (2) a jamming-aware framework that first identifies the jamming type, applies coarse jamming cancellation (CJC) for high-power jamming, then applies diffusion-based fine denoising. The paper's own contribution is the DEFENSIVE framework; the attack taxonomy in Section III is a survey/framing of prior threats.
threat_model: Wireless semantic-communication link between transmitter Alice and legitimate receiver Bob, with the trained semantic encoder/decoder (and joint source-channel coder) publicly released. Adversary Eve/Jammer. Eavesdropping branch: Eve passively intercepts Alice's signal and runs (a) model inversion attacks (reconstructs data by inverting the public models) or (b) inference attacks — membership inference (is X in the dataset) or attribute inference (age, gender, health, image labels). Eve's 'knowledge' = her DL model, parameters, and specific eavesdropping task. Jamming branch: an active jammer either (a) high-power jamming (e.g., pulse jamming at 40 dB JSR) to overpower the signal, or (b) low-power adversarial attack (<10% of signal power, JSR -10 dB) crafted (FGSM/BIM/PGD-style) to fool Bob's semantic decoder. Defender goals are three-way trade-off: Security (starve Eve of information), Reliability (Bob decodes correctly), Covertness (channel input barely changes before/after AN so Eve cannot detect the defense).
limitations: Position/magazine-style article: no ablations, no numeric result tables (findings read off figures), and no formal algorithm or convergence analysis. Evaluated on a single toy dataset (Fashion-MNIST) and image-reconstruction task only. Assumes additive (AWGN) channels; the authors explicitly flag fading (multiplicative) channels as unsolved because the transmission process no longer aligns with the diffusion forward process. Covers only the deployment phase — training-phase threats (backdoor, poisoning) are named as future work, not addressed. Reliability of the core time-step estimation step (matching received-signal SNR/SINR to the variance schedule beta_t) is asserted but not quantified under estimation error. Diffusion training/inference cost on resource-constrained edge devices is acknowledged as a drawback (pruning/distributed/task-agnostic designs left to future work). NOTE ON MIS-BUCKETING: the feed's 'Membership Inference Attacks' cluster label overstates the paper's scope — membership inference is only one leaf of the Section III eavesdropping taxonomy (cited to refs [11],[12]) and is NOT the studied attack; the eavesdropping case study actually uses an ATTRIBUTE inference attack (recovering image labels).

quantitative_results: Eavesdropping Part A (Alice lacks Eve knowledge, Gaussian AN): privacy-leakage mutual information of the Alice-Eve link driven to near zero; perceptibility MSE kept well below the upper bound (all Alice power to AN)

Eavesdropping Part B (Alice knows Eve, adversarial-attack AN): Eve's classification accuracy dropped to ~0.2 (near-ineffective); perceptibility MSE consistently below 0.1 with transmitter output power normalized to 1; adversarial-attack power < 10% of the original transmitted signal power
Eavesdropping setup: Alice-Bob SNR fixed at 5 dB; Alice-Eve SNR varied from -5 dB to 20 dB

Jamming high-power case: pulse jamming at JSR 40 dB causes Bob to fail image reconstruction; direct diffusion reverse process reduces MSE but stays high; coarse jamming cancellation + fine diffusion denoising reduces communication MSE close to the no-jamming level
Jamming low-power case: adversarial attack at JSR -10 dB sharply worsens Alice-Bob MSE; diffusion reverse process significantly improves MSE, with larger gains as SNR decreases
Jamming setup: Alice-Bob SNR varied from -5 dB to 20 dB
Noise-estimation neural network: four-layer fully connected network
baselines_compared: Original semantic communication system (no AN / no denoising)
Upper bound: all of Alice's transmission power allocated to artificial noise (perceptibility reference)
Direct diffusion reverse process vs. coarse jamming cancellation (CJC) + fine diffusion denoising
